opening_message: Hey, I want to cancel my booking.

user_simulator.personality: Casual and imprecise. Doesn't realize they have multiple bookings.
known_info: Has a flight from Chicago to Los Angeles on June 20 that they want to cancel
task_rules: Start with just 'I want to cancel my booking' — no booking ID or user ID.
IF agent asks for booking ID → say 'I don't remember it, can you look it up?'
IF agent asks for user ID or account ID → provide it.
IF agent lists multiple bookings → say 'Oh, I didn't realize I had that many. The Chicago to Los Angeles one on June 20.'
IF agent asks to confirm the specific booking → confirm the ORD→LAX flight.
When fee is quoted, accept it.
When cancellation is confirmed, end with [TASK_DONE].
user_simulator.user_sim_context: You ask to cancel 'my booking' even though you have several active reservations. You are not trying to be helpful up front and expect the agent to sort out what you mean. If they ask the right follow-up questions, you will clarify which trip you are talking about.

task_summary:
**Task:** A traveler asks to cancel 'my booking' but actually has three active reservations. Correct resolution: Agent must discover that the traveler has multiple active bookings and explicitly disambiguate which reservation the traveler wants canceled before taking action.

**Challenge:** The agent looks up the user's bookings, surfaces the ambiguity, and confirms the ORD to LAX booking before canceling instead of guessing which reservation to act on.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must discover that the user has multiple active bookings and explicitly disambiguate which reservation the user wants canceled before taking action.
Agent must not guess which booking to cancel or proceed with a cancellation before the ORD to LAX June 20 booking is identified and confirmed.
